data.author: robwoolley
data.message:
Simplify removing leading and trailing separators (#152)

Previously, we checked if the leading or trailing character was a colon
and then used a wildcard to remove it.

It is simpler to just remove a leading or trailing colon. This has the
added benefit of only using shell built-in functions.

Signed-off-by: Rob Woolley <rob.woolley@windriver.com>

data.author: IsabelParedes
data.message:
Migrate from legacy importlib-resources (#143)

* Migrate from legacy importlib-resources

The path() function is deprecated.
https://importlib-resources.readthedocs.io/en/latest/using.html#migrating-from-legacy
For Python>=3.9, use files() instead.

* Use path() when files() is not available

Signed-off-by: Isabel Paredes <isabel.paredes@quantstack.net>
Co-authored-by: Chris Lalancette <clalancette@gmail.com>
